title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: Complete the text with ONE word only.

content.text: Here I am in a cold, windy city, 0 under a very grey sky. I ask myself 9 why I'm standing on an oversized surfboard in the middle of a river with nothing to help me 10 but a paddle. I'm about to have my first lesson in paddle-boarding, which is a bit 11 like canoeing but with only one paddle and, being upright, you can enjoy the views on offer. The teacher reassures me it's easy, which 12 does nothing to reduce the pressure. I desperately try to keep 13 my balance and concentrate on not falling in. I wonder if I've left it too late to back out and head for solid ground, but before I can change my mind I'm 14 on the move, but not going where I want to. I hear my teacher shouting 'Paddle paddle'; I try but, 15 despite my best efforts, I don't make much progress. 'You need to paddle on both sides,' he says, 'because 16 otherwise you'll go around in circles. Copy me.' And finally I'm moving in the same direction as everyone else and it feels amazing.

questions.explanation: The author is clearly puzzled by the situation he finds himself in or doubts his motives for doing that.
A paddle is the only thing the author has in his hands.
A comparison between two activities is made: paddle-boarding and canoeing.
Teacher's reassurance has no effect on the narrator. It does nothing to him.
No article is needed here as 'balance' is an abstract noun.
'On the move' means 'in a state of moving, in motion'.
A negative preposition is needed to show contrast between the author's action and its result.
Not rowing on both sides results in going in circles.
